posts.title: Microstructure as a Map, Not a Mystery
posts.excerpt: A short note on reading grains, phases, and interfaces as the city plan of a material.
posts.body:
Microstructure is often introduced as something to observe, but I like treating it as something to navigate. Grain boundaries, phase contrast, pores, twins, and precipitates are all clues about what happened during processing and what might happen under service.

This blog section is ready for longer research notes, images from experiments, conference reflections, and polished explainers.

posts.title: Processing Leaves Fingerprints
posts.excerpt: Cooling rate, heat treatment, and fabrication route all leave evidence if you know where to look.
posts.body: A material remembers its processing history. The challenge is to connect that memory to measurable structure and useful performance. I want this site to become a place where those connections can be sketched clearly.

posts.title: Why I Like Research Visuals
posts.excerpt: Good figures can turn a difficult materials question into something people can reason about together.
posts.body: A good research visual is not decoration. It is a thinking tool. It lets people compare, argue, remember, and improve an idea. That is the spirit of this homepage: technically serious, but visually alive.
